title: pittosporum daphniphylloides var. daphniphylloides
intro: Pittosporum daphniphylloides var. daphniphylloides, also known as Kohuhu, is a plant species native to New Zealand and Australia, belonging to the Pittosporaceae family. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are used in traditional medicine, exhibiting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Kohuhu include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. Kohuhu is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil for topical application, although its use may require proper guidance and caution due to potential side effects.
